what_it_does: Defines window.MS_STREAMS, a registry and runtime model for declarative, ordered step sequences called streams. It declares step kinds, synthesize phases, five concrete stream definitions, and helper functions for visibility, gating, progress, validation, brief construction, and a tiny mutable run controller.
observations: A stream is an ordered list of steps whose kind determines which external registry the step references: decisions, kinds, preferences, gather/review, or adaptive ask/synthesize moves.
Five concrete streams are defined: init, material, diagnostic, deck, and widget, with an ORDER array controlling listing order.
Step kinds include gather, preference, decision, options, produce, review, ask, and synthesize; synthesize steps map to SYNTH_PHASES: direction, renderer, sequence, ready.
Adaptive ask steps carry a when(answers) guard and a move payload describing field, kind, options, Vi prompt, helper text, and recommendation logic.
briefFromAnswers walks visible ask steps and builds the project brief consumed by MS_SYNTH_AI, folding in extra sources, goal notes, and density.
validate checks that decision refs resolve in MS_DECISIONS, kind refs in MS_KINDS, and synthesize refs in SYNTH_PHASES.
forSurface binds streams to page/surface identifiers via the surfaces array on each stream.
understanding and canProduce compute readiness from answered gating steps; nextStep finds the first visible step not yet handled or answered.
standouts: The same stream abstraction is used for full project initialization and for focused in-project sub-sequences.
Adaptive ask steps reuse MS_INTAKE move descriptors, bringing intake logic into the unified runner without duplicating it.
briefFromAnswers is the explicit seam between captured stream answers and the synthesis brief.
Runtime validation ties stream definitions to live external registries.
conventions: Uppercase constants for registries and the helper factory s(...) for step objects.
Defensive getters log errors and return null for unknown stream, step kind, or synthesize phase ids.
Pure helper functions for computation plus a single mutable run controller.
Step gating is inferred from kind unless optional or required flags override it.
concerns: run mutates an internal ord variable in closure despite the comment framing helpers as pure.
isGating does not include options steps, so canProduce may permit production before configuration is answered.
validate depends on global window.MS_DECISIONS and window.MS_KINDS being loaded; it may report false problems if registries are absent.
briefFromAnswers only folds ask steps into the brief, ignoring decision, preference, and options answers.
nextStep requires an external handled Set to mark gather/produce/review completion, leaving correctness partly with the caller.
The done helper is defined locally inside nextStep and is not exposed for reuse or testing.
notes: This file is the declarative flow engine: it turns step metadata into a runnable, gating-aware sequence and builds the brief that feeds synthesis.
questions: How is the handled Set for non-gating steps populated and persisted across runner sessions?
What is the relationship between the stream/stream_step tables mentioned in the header and the in-memory run controller?
Are options steps intentionally non-gating, or should configuration block produce?
How do decision and preference answers get folded into the brief beyond the extra.density fallback?
Is run meant to be reconstructed from persisted ordinal, or is it only ephemeral UI state?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a unifying declarative stream mechanism for initialization and sub-sequences; the file matches that purpose, providing registry, validation, progress, and brief construction. It also quietly acts as the binding layer to MS_INTAKE moves and MS_SYNTH_AI phases.
apparent_themes: One mechanism for full-project onboarding and focused regeneration.
Adaptive questioning driven by prior answers.
Separation of flow orchestration from artifact generation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make project onboarding and in-project regeneration feel like one consistent, conversational wizard by expressing every flow as a reusable, validated, step-kind-typed sequence that generic surfaces can render.
novelty: The unification of intake wizard, material add, diagnostic, deck, and widget flows into a single stream registry with adaptive ask-moves and a synthesize sub-phase mapping is the distinctive design here.
contribution: A declarative stream model and runtime API that surfaces can use to render step sequences, compute readiness, validate references, and convert captured answers into a synthesis brief without hardcoding each flow.
summary_for_embedding: MS_STREAMS is a declarative flow registry and runtime for Counterpart's step-based project initialization and in-project regeneration sequences, defining step kinds, synthesize phases, stream schemas, and helper functions for visibility, gating, progress, validation, and brief construction.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, reusable stream abstraction that drives both the initial project wizard and focused sub-sequences by composing existing decision, kind, preference, and synthesis registries.

symbols.does: Factory helper that builds a step object from positional arguments.
symbols.description: Creates a step record with id, kind, title, optional ref and sub text, and merges any extra metadata such as railLabel, when, or move payloads.

symbols.does: Returns streams optionally filtered by scope.
symbols.description: Maps ORDER to stream definitions and filters by the provided scope, returning a list of stream metadata objects.

symbols.does: Determines whether a captured answer value counts as provided.
symbols.description: Treats null/undefined as unanswered, empty arrays as unanswered, whitespace-only strings as unanswered, and any other value as answered.

symbols.does: Filters a stream's steps by each step's when(answers) guard.
symbols.description: Returns the subset of steps whose adaptive predicate is satisfied by the current answer map, defaulting to visible if no predicate exists.

symbols.does: Decides whether a step blocks downstream progress until answered.
symbols.description: Returns true if the step is explicitly required, or if it is an ask, decision, or preference step that is not explicitly optional.

symbols.does: Computes how complete the answered gating steps are.
symbols.description: Calculates an overall ratio, counts of answered and gating steps, and a per-step answered map for the visible steps of a stream.

symbols.does: Checks whether the stream may advance to its first produce step.
symbols.description: Requires every gating step before the first produce step to be answered; if no produce step exists, it gates on all visible gating steps.

symbols.does: Finds the first visible step still needing attention.
symbols.description: Returns the first visible step that is neither in the handled set nor an already-answered gating step, or null if none remain.

symbols.does: Local predicate inside nextStep for whether a step is finished.
symbols.description: Returns true if the step id is in the handled Set or if the step is gating and has a valid answer.

symbols.does: Looks up a stream by id with error logging.
symbols.description: Returns the stream definition or null if unknown, logging the known ORDER list to the console.

symbols.does: Builds the synthesis brief from captured stream answers.
symbols.description: Walks visible ask steps, writes each move.field from the answer map, and folds in extra sources, goal notes, and density to produce the brief object.

symbols.does: Looks up metadata for a synthesize sub-phase by ref.
symbols.description: Returns the phase label, icon, and description from SYNTH_PHASES, or null with an error if the phase is unknown.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of a stream's step list.
symbols.description: Uses get to resolve the stream and returns a copy of its steps array, or an empty array if the stream is unknown.

symbols.does: Lists streams launchable from a given page/surface id.
symbols.description: Filters all streams whose surfaces array includes the supplied page identifier.

symbols.does: Looks up metadata for a step kind.
symbols.description: Returns the label, icon, and description from STEP_KINDS, or null with an error if the kind is unknown.

symbols.does: Returns a copy of the STEP_KINDS registry.
symbols.description: Uses Object.assign to return a shallow clone of the step-kind metadata map.

symbols.does: Checks a stream's steps for unresolved references and bad kinds.
symbols.description: Reports problems for unknown step kinds, unresolvable decision refs, kind refs, and synthesize phase refs by consulting external registries.

symbols.does: Returns the step at a given ordinal.
symbols.description: Indexes into the stream's steps array and returns the step or null if out of bounds.

symbols.does: Computes progress metadata for an ordinal.
symbols.description: Returns the clamped index, total step count, completion ratio, and start/end flags for the current position in a stream.

symbols.does: Clamps an ordinal to the stream's step bounds.
symbols.description: Returns the ordinal constrained between 0 and the last valid step index.

symbols.does: Creates a tiny mutable stream controller.
symbols.description: Returns an object exposing the stream, current step, progress, next/previous/goto navigation, and the current ordinal; navigation mutates an internal ord variable.
